political.s: <cite index="1-3">Zoetis is the world's largest producer of medicine and vaccinations for pets and livestock.</cite> FEC records show the company donated $138K in the 2024 cycle with bipartisan distribution (22% Republican, 57% Democratic), indicating balanced political engagement across both parties.

charity.s: <cite index="24-2">The Zoetis Foundation dispersed $11.3 million in grants in 2024, including funding for nearly 500 veterinary student scholarships.</cite> <cite index="25-6">The Foundation has distributed $26.2 million since 2021, supporting education, well-being and livelihoods for veterinarians and farmers as well as humanitarian aid and a matching gifts program.</cite>
charity.focus: Veterinary education, farmer livelihoods, animal care access, disaster relief

environment.s: <cite index="24-8">Zoetis sourced 80.6% of global electricity from renewable sources and advanced sustainable manufacturing with packaging improvements and reduced emissions.</cite> <cite index="25-17,25-18">The company reduced total Scope 1 and 2 emissions by 26.7% from 2023, primarily driven by increased renewable energy credits procured through a virtual power purchase agreement.</cite>

labor.s: <cite index="34-1,34-2">72% of Zoetis employees would recommend working there to a friend based on Glassdoor reviews, with ratings of 3.9 out of 5 for work life balance, 3.7 for culture and values and 3.4 for career opportunities.</cite> However, employee reviews indicate mixed experiences with management quality and workplace culture. Pending California wage/hour litigation suggests compliance concerns.

dei.s: <cite index="52-2,52-3">Zoetis has been named to Fair360's Top 50 Companies list for the first time, with Zoetis being the only animal health company included in this year's Top 50 rankings.</cite> <cite index="53-7,53-8,53-9">The company launched its Awareness & Action Drive Impact initiative in January 2020, positioning DEI as one of five strategic priorities, and created the role of Chief Talent, Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion Officer with a team that expanded from three to thirteen.</cite>

animals.s: <cite index="14-1">Zoetis is on the Cruelty Free Investing animal use list due to its use of animal testing in development and testing of its products.</cite> <cite index="11-8,11-10">The company is committed to cross-industry efforts to develop in vitro testing alternatives and to developing and use scientifically validated alternative testing methods deemed acceptable to regulatory authorities.</cite>

guns.s: As an animal health pharmaceutical company, Zoetis does not manufacture or sell firearms.

privacy.s: <cite index="73-1,73-4">Zoetis is committed to the responsible, ethical, and transparent use of personal data, with its Global Privacy Office maintaining an enterprise-wide policy and supporting procedures that guide the collection, maintenance and protection of personal data.</cite> <cite index="73-10,73-11">The Zoetis information security program is aligned against the National Institute of Standards and Technology Cybersecurity Framework and employs a risk-based, intelligence-driven program with multiple defenses and layers of security.</cite>

execPay.s: <cite index="68-1">CEO Kristin Peck made $15,237,853 in total compensation during 2023.</cite> <cite index="65-2">The average estimated annual salary at Zoetis is $119,938, or $57 per hour.</cite> This represents a CEO-to-median employee pay ratio of approximately 127:1.

fec.committeeName: ZOETIS INC. PAC AKA ZOETIS PAC

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
